evals.prompt: I have an empty project directory and want to start using linked-intent development on it. Please set it up. Use Full LID.

assertions.text: docs/intent/ directory exists in the project
docs/specs/ directory was NOT created (specs live inside node folders under docs/intent/, not in a separate top-level directory)
docs/high-level-design.md exists, populated from the HLD template (not empty)
docs/planning/ directory was NOT created
AGENTS.md contains a bare '## LID' heading followed by a '- Mode: Full' bullet (mode is a bullet, not merged into the heading)
AGENTS.md contains the LID directives block referencing 'linked-intent-dev'
A CLAUDE.md alias exists resolving to AGENTS.md's content — either a symlink to AGENTS.md or a CLAUDE.md whose sole content is the line '@AGENTS.md' — so Claude Code reads the same instructions
The agent's response summarizes what files were created or modified

evals.prompt: I already have a CLAUDE.md with my team's existing conventions. I want to adopt linked-intent development without losing any of my existing content. Set it up.

files.content:
# My Project

## Conventions

- Prefer functional style where possible.
- Use TypeScript strict mode.
- Don't add dependencies without discussion.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md still contains all of the original content ('# My Project', '## Conventions', and the three bullet points) unmodified
CLAUDE.md has the LID directives block appended (contains 'linked-intent-dev' or 'Linked-Intent Development')
CLAUDE.md contains a '## LID' block with a '- Mode:' bullet
No AGENTS.md was created — the project already centered CLAUDE.md, so it was reconciled in place rather than migrated to AGENTS.md
The agent's response explicitly summarizes which sections were added to CLAUDE.md

evals.prompt: Please set up LID in this project.

files.content:

- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

**Consult the `linked-intent-dev` skill for ALL code changes.** All changes flow through the arrow of intent in one direction:

```
HLD → LLDs → EARS → Tests → Code
```

# High-Level Design

## Problem

*(not yet specified)*

assertions.text: No file changes were made (CLAUDE.md and existing docs are unmodified)
The agent's response informs the user what was detected (mode, version, directory status) rather than silently doing nothing

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md now contains a bare '## LID' heading on its own line (the heading is no longer the malformed '## LID Mode: Full')
CLAUDE.md now contains a standalone '- Mode: Full' bullet under the '## LID' heading

evals.prompt: Please reconcile this project's LID setup against current conventions.

files.content:
# My Project

## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.2.0

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

**Consult the `linked-intent-dev` skill for ALL code changes.**

# High-Level Design

## Problem

Payments and orders for a small storefront.

---
parent: high-level-design
prefix: ORDER
---
# LLD: Orders

Order lifecycle.

# orders specs

**LLD**: docs/intent/orders/orders-design.md

- `[x]` **ORDER-001**: The system SHALL create an order.

# LLD: Order Refunds

A separate LLD for the refund sub-flow, left as an extra file in the orders folder instead of relocated into its own child node folder.

# orders refund specs

- `[x]` **REFUND-001**: The system SHALL refund an order.

---
parent: high-level-design
prefix: [PAY, FEE]
---
# LLD: Payments

Handles charges and processing fees.

# payments specs

**LLD**: docs/intent/payments/payments-design.md

- `[x]` **PAY-001**: The system SHALL charge a card.
- `[x]` **FEE-001**: The system SHALL record a processing fee.

assertions.text: The agent surfaces that docs/intent/orders/ holds more than its <node>-design.md + <node>-specs.md pair (the extra orders-refund-design.md / orders-refund-specs.md files), flagging it as an LLD not yet relocated into its own node folder
For the overloaded orders folder, the agent recommends a concrete resolution (e.g. promote the refund LLD into its own child folder docs/intent/orders/order-refund/, making orders a sub-HLD)

The agent also surfaces that docs/intent/payments/payments-design.md carries a 'prefix:' array ([PAY, FEE]) as an unresolved marker, and recommends a resolution (collapse into <LEAF>-<TYPE> facets, promote, or split)
The agent surfaces both markers even though the project's '- Version:' already equals the installed version (drift detected independently of version lag)
The agent surfaces these as proposed updates requiring confirmation rather than silently rewriting the files or silently leaving the markers unmentioned

evals.prompt: This project records LID Version 1.1.0 but I want it on the current 1.2.0 conventions. Please reconcile it. If a structural decision needs my judgment, surface it with a recommendation but defer it for now and proceed — do not block on it.

files.content:
# My Project

## LID
- Mode: Full
- Version: 1.1.0

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

**Consult the `linked-intent-dev` skill for ALL code changes.**

# Changelog

## [1.2.0]

### Migration (v1.1 → v1.2)
- Node-as-folder: each LLD lives in docs/intent/<node>/ as <node>-design.md (plus <node>-specs.md).
- A design doc whose `prefix:` frontmatter is an array is an unresolved multi-prefix marker; resolve by collapse into <LEAF>-<TYPE> facets, promote to a sub-HLD over child leaves, or split into sibling leaves. A `prefix:` array must not survive the walk.

# High-Level Design

## Problem

Subscription billing for a SaaS product.

---
parent: high-level-design
prefix: [BILL, INVOICE]
---
# LLD: Billing

Charges, invoices, and dunning.

# billing specs

**LLD**: docs/intent/billing/billing-design.md

- `[x]` **BILL-001**: The system SHALL charge a subscription.
- `[x]` **INVOICE-001**: The system SHALL issue an invoice.

assertions.text: CLAUDE.md's '- Version:' bullet has been advanced to 1.2.0 (the installed version) even though a structural judgment step was deferred (the version is NOT left pinned at 1.1.0)
The agent's report explicitly names the deferred/outstanding item — the 'prefix:' array ([BILL, INVOICE]) on docs/intent/billing/billing-design.md — as a persisting marker still to resolve
The agent recommended a resolution for the deferred prefix array (collapse into <LEAF>-<TYPE> facets, promote, or split) rather than leaving it unmentioned
The agent did NOT silently rewrite or auto-resolve the prefix array — docs/intent/billing/billing-design.md still contains 'prefix: [BILL, INVOICE]'

assertions.text: The skill detected the existing LID configuration by reading AGENTS.md (no CLAUDE.md is present), confirming it treats AGENTS.md as the instruction file when present

evals.prompt: I have an empty project directory and want to start using linked-intent development on it. Please set it up. Use Full LID.

assertions.text: AGENTS.md contains a tool-agnostic memory-to-intent directive instructing the agent that, before saving durable project knowledge to agent/tool memory, it tests whether the knowledge is project intent and, if so, records it in the arrow rather than memory

The memory-to-intent directive is phrased tool-agnostically (it does not name a specific product's memory feature) and distinguishes project intent (which goes to the arrow) from user/relationship knowledge (which legitimately stays in memory)
